title: Stack Trace Analysis
description: Parse and analyze Python stack traces represented as strings. Extract the error type and message from the last line, count the number of stack frames, and extract file/line/function information from the deepest frame in the traceback.
starterCode:
def extract_error_type(traceback_str):
    """Extract the error type from a traceback string.

    The error type is the name before the colon on the last line,
    e.g., 'ValueError' from 'ValueError: invalid literal'.
    If there is no colon, the entire last line is the error type.

    Args:
        traceback_str: A multi-line traceback string.

    Returns:
        The error type as a string (e.g., 'ValueError').
    """
    pass


def extract_error_message(traceback_str):
    """Extract the error message from a traceback string.

    The message is everything after the first colon on the last line.
    Returns empty string if there is no colon (no message).

    Args:
        traceback_str: A multi-line traceback string.

    Returns:
        The error message string, stripped of leading/trailing whitespace.
    """
    pass


def count_frames(traceback_str):
    """Count the number of stack frames in a traceback.

    Each frame starts with a line containing 'File "' pattern.

    Args:
        traceback_str: A multi-line traceback string.

    Returns:
        The number of stack frames (int).
    """
    pass


def get_error_location(traceback_str):
    """Extract location info from the deepest (last) stack frame.

    Parses the last 'File "...", line N, in func' line.

    Args:
        traceback_str: A multi-line traceback string.

    Returns:
        A dict with keys 'file' (str), 'line' (int), 'function' (str).
        Returns {'file': '', 'line': 0, 'function': ''} if no frame found.
    """
    pass
hints: The error type and message are always on the last line of the traceback. Split on ':' to separate them.
Use re.findall() with the pattern 'File "' to count stack frames — each frame starts with this pattern.
Use re.findall(r'File "([^"]+)", line (\d+), in (\S+)', tb) to extract file, line number, and function name from each frame.

testCases.description: extract_error_type returns the exception class name
testCases.validation:
tb = 'Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "app.py", line 10, in main
    x = int(val)
ValueError: invalid literal'; assert extract_error_type(tb) == 'ValueError'

testCases.description: extract_error_message returns the message after the error type
testCases.validation:
tb = 'Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "app.py", line 5, in parse
    return int(s)
ValueError: invalid literal for int()'; assert extract_error_message(tb) == 'invalid literal for int()'

testCases.description: count_frames counts File lines in the traceback
testCases.validation:
tb = 'Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "a.py", line 1, in f
    func()
  File "b.py", line 2, in g
    x = 1/0
ZeroDivisionError: division by zero'; assert count_frames(tb) == 2

testCases.description: get_error_location returns info from the deepest frame
testCases.validation:
tb = 'Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "main.py", line 10, in main
    result = calc()
  File "calc.py", line 5, in calculate
    return 1/0
ZeroDivisionError: division by zero'; loc = get_error_location(tb); assert loc == {'file': 'calc.py', 'line': 5, 'function': 'calculate'}

testCases.description: Single frame traceback is handled correctly
testCases.validation:
tb = 'Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 3, in <module>
    print(x)
NameError: name x is not defined'; assert count_frames(tb) == 1 and extract_error_type(tb) == 'NameError'

testCases.description: Error without message returns empty string
testCases.validation:
tb = 'Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "main.py", line 1, in <module>
    raise KeyboardInterrupt
KeyboardInterrupt'; assert extract_error_type(tb) == 'KeyboardInterrupt' and extract_error_message(tb) == ''
